title: cyphokentia cerifera
intro: Cyphokentia cerifera, also known as the 'Wax Palm', is a species of palm tree native to New Guinea. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and sap, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cyphokentia cerifera include relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Cyphokentia cerifera include infusions and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
